violations.text: The establishment does not employ a full-time, on-site manager certified in food safety at all times when time temperature control for safety (TCS) food is being prepared, handled, and/or served. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 03/24/2026

Raw animal foods (such as eggs and chicken) were observed stored above ready-to-eat foods (such as produce and cooked foods) in the walk-in refrigerators. Raw animal foods must be separated from raw ready-to-eat food such as vegetables during storage. PIC relocated raw items. Corrected On-Site. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 03/24/2026

Empanada was observed held at 128 degrees Fahrenheit. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be maintained at 135 degrees Fahrenheit or above except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. PIC reheated. Corrected On-Site. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 03/24/2026

The establishment does not employ a full-time, on-site manager certified in food safety at all times when time temperature control for safety (TCS) food is being prepared, handled, and/or served. New Violation. Correct By: 03/10/2026
The hand-washing sink in the restroom was lacking paper towels. PIC replaced. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 03/10/2026
Inspector observed a package of cheese and container of garlic with mold. PIC discarded. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 03/10/2026

Raw animal foods (such as eggs and raw chicken) were observed stored above ready-to-eat foods (such as deli meat) in the walk-in refrigerators. Raw animal foods must be separated from raw ready-to-eat food such as vegetables during storage. PIC relocated raw items. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 03/10/2026

Soup, which was prepared in advance, was not cooled to 70 degrees Fahrenheit in 2 hours and then to 41 degrees Fahrenheit within 4 hours. PIC discarded. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 03/10/2026

Empanadas were observed held between 122-130 degrees Fahrenheit. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be maintained at 135 degrees Fahrenheit or above except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. PIC reheated and turned up hot-hold unit. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 03/10/2026

Various items were observed held at between 49-52 degrees Fahrenheit. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. PIC relocated cheese and eggs and discarded other items. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 03/10/2026
Working containers used for storing chemicals taken from bulk supplies were not marked with the common name of the chemical. PIC labeled. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 03/10/2026

Chlorine sanitizer solution was observed in excess of 200 parts per million (ppm). A chlorine sanitizer solution applied to food contact surfaces must be between 50-100 ppm. PIC remade solution and retested. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 03/10/2026

Proper cooling methods were not used for time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods. Inspector observed soup cooling in the walk-in cooler from the previous day, in a container deeper than 4 inches, with a lid full of condensation, and a temperature of 47F. PIC discarded. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 03/10/2026
Inspector observed walk-in thermometer in poor repair (inaccurate ambient temperature). PIC replaced. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 03/10/2026
The facility is lacking test strips to measure the concentration of the quat and chlorine sanitizing solution for the 3 bay sink and spray bottle. New Violation. Correct By: 03/10/2026
The produce walk-in unit is in poor repair (ambient 50F).The physical facilities must be maintained in good repair. New Violation. Correct By: 03/10/2026
